paragraphs.body: Is Andrew Tate defendable? Pro Vs Con Diagram

Pro: Tate gives advice that can help young men, especially those with depression or low self- esteem. He helps these young men to regain their confidence. Although it is controversial, he represents an anti-misandrist position, which is what young men want to hear when they feel that feminists are making them feel guilty just for being men. Tate also speaks out for men’s problems such as “male suicide, masculinity, lack of fatherhood.”

annotations.text: nce. Although it is controversial, he represents an anti-misandrist position, which is what young men want to hear when they feel that feminists are making them feel guilty just for being men. Tate also speaks out for
annotations.comment: The writing here is fine, and it's clear you are developing Tate's personality through describing his behavior, but at moments like this, using concrete and specific details would help to develop what, exactly, Tate promotes and how he goes about it.

paragraphs.body: Con: Many women argue that Tate overstates his masculinity to the point of being misogynistic. His goal is probably just to recruit more male followers by being provocative, but it is very easy to see what he says as discriminatory. The following quote can be seen as blood-boiling, but it aptly captures this point, “You can’t be responsible for a dog if it doesn’t obey you, or a woman that doesn’t obey you.” Tate might be thinking that if you tell a dog to not go outside and it goes outside and gets attacked by other dogs, it is not the owner’s fault. By extension, if a woman goes to a bar alone against the advice of her boyfriend and is assaulted, then her boyfriend should not be held responsible for the hospital bill. This argument may seem logical to men, but, in the end, Tate is making a comparison of women to dogs, and the only thing he succeeds in is provoking people and pitting them against each other.

Pro: Tate promotes an entrepreneurial spirit. He does this by encouraging a warrior-like mindset. Instead of shying away from challenges, he tackles them head on aggressively. He also encourages discipline and resilience. He does not allow setbacks to deter him from succeeding. On top of this, he demonstrates an organized mindset by setting clear and ambitious goals, one step at a time, without overwhelming the audience.

Many people in the LGBTQIA+ community perceive Tate as homophobic. While Tate does not make explicitly homophobic claims, he is insidiously dismissive of gay people in that he uses the word “gay” as an insult. When he does not like something, he nonchalantly calls it gay. From a gay person’s perspective, this is easy to see as extremely offensive because Tate questions the identity of gay people as one that is value neutral. Tate routinely makes insulting remarks such as “that's gay and fake,” which, again, suggests that he does not recognize a minority sexual orientation as legitimate in its own right. He insinuates that being gay equals being fake.

paragraphs.body: Pro: He promotes good health and the cultivation of a strong body. He does this by promoting body strength and healthiness. He promotes masculinity by encouraging men to develop stronger muscles. He also posts his own exercise routines and videos for people to watch and learn from, alongside his own purchasable courses.

Con: Tate is not just a provocateur influencer, but he is also involved in morally reprehensible activities. He has admitted that in the past he talked to lonely men online pretending to be a woman, indirectly inferring that if they send him money, he may meet with them or just give them more attention. He then proceeded to hire a multitude of women to talk to rich lonely men online and convince them to send him money. Some of these men sent him money, even larger amounts like $25,000. This is indefensible and this is why he was justifiably charged with human trafficking. Tate said he paid these women to do the work, but even then the work was morally questionable and while not illegal, taking advantage of lonely men is unacceptable.

Conclusion: In the end, Andrew Tate is a divisive figure and I do not think that he is defensible. In a political climate in which politicians and the media practice an “us versus them” approach to politics, dialogue would be more important than insisting on one’s truth. This dialogue needs to happen not only between Democrats and Republicans, but also between women and men.

annotations.comment: Hello, The central focusing idea of this piece is a measured and thoughtful report on the pros and cons of Andrew Tate’s persona. You do a really great job here of playing the Believing Game—by which I mean that you are presenting facts and information about Tate without revealing whether you agree with the pros or cons. This can be particularly difficult for anybody to do, especially if they already have an opinion about the topic. The ability to do this is a great strength. The terms that stand out the most to me here are all the variations of the words associated with gender—man, woman, masculinity, feminist, and so forth. It does seem clear, given this brief piece of writing, that Andrew Tate has a lot of very specific ideas about very stringent and polarized roles/rules for men and women. It makes me feel a little frustrated, because I don’t like when people elect themselves to police other people’s gender. It sounds like Tate like to work out—that’s great! Good for him—but that doesn’t mean his workouts are best for every man. Nor does it mean women can’t enjoy workouts similar to him as well. The audience for this piece would likely be young folks who are interested in the current conversation regarding gender in America and worldwide. The writing here clearly indicates Tate’s interest in what many people believe is a traditional approach to gender roles. The purpose of the writing is to clearly state the opinion that Andrew Tate’s actions are unacceptable. At this point, however, the writing is much more like a report than an essay. I do appreciate the way you formalized your opinion in the final paragraph, but your reader doesn’t get to see any of you throughout the writing. I wonder if you have any personal observations/experiences that you could include that would allow you to engage with Tate on a more personal level. I believe you could maintain the pros and cons aspects of the writing while making it much more of an essay about you as well. As I said earlier, it is a real talent to be able to thoughtfully and thoroughly present opposing opinions about any topic, and I learned from reading this piece that you have those skills and are very capable of using the to the benefit of your writing. Not an easy thing to do. Very well done. Thank you for This,
